title: Common Problems in Software Development
subtitle: Many businesses face the same challenges when building systems.

items.title: Rebuilding the Same System Again and Again
items.description: Each project starts from scratch without a reusable foundation, leading to repeated time and cost inefficiencies.

items.title: Systems That Don’t Scale
items.description: Applications work in the early stage but start to break down as the business grows because they were not designed for scalability.

items.title: Messy and Hard-to-Maintain Codebase
items.description: Unstructured systems make it difficult to maintain, extend, and onboard new developers without introducing risks.

items.title: Business Logic Scattered Everywhere
items.description: Core business logic is not properly structured, making it hard to control, test, and evolve over time.

items.title: No Long-Term Technical Direction
items.description: Projects move forward without a clear long-term vision, making every change more complex and expensive.

items.title: Over-Reliance on Short-Term Solutions
items.description: Quick fixes without proper planning eventually turn into technical debt that slows down future development.
